title: Fluffy Earthy Moroccan Lamb Mechoui
description: A hearty and flavorful Moroccan dish featuring lamb, unwaxed lemon, and earthy spices, perfect for a post-workout meal. This shojin-friendly recipe is made with a tadka and served in a mechoui form.
instructions: Preheat the oven to 300 degrees Fahrenheit.
Rinse the lamb shoulder and pat it dry with paper towels.
In a small bowl, mix together the olive oil, cumin, smoked paprika, coriander, salt, and black pepper.
Rub the spice mixture all over the lamb shoulder, making sure to coat it evenly.
Heat a large Dutch oven over medium-high heat and sear the lamb shoulder until browned on all sides.
Add the garlic and cook for 1 minute, until fragrant.
Add the unwaxed lemon juice and bring the mixture to a simmer.
Cover the Dutch oven with a lid and transfer it to the preheated oven.
Braise the lamb shoulder for 2.5 hours, until it is tender and falls apart easily.
Remove the lamb shoulder from the oven and let it rest for 10 minutes before slicing it thinly.
Garnish with chopped fresh parsley and serve hot.
